items.content:

items.title: 'The carnage needs to end' — UN chief seeks to increase international pressure on Israel | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Gaza health officials say more than a dozen people have been killed on Saturday, as Israel presses ahead with its latest offensive in Gaza City.  International pressure has been mounting on Israel over its deadly operation, with Portugal becoming the latest country to announce it will recognize Palestinian statehood.  The United Nations is due to convene a special session on a two-state solution on the sidelines of next week's General Assembly. UN Secretary General Antonio Guterres says the gathering is a chance to 'mobilize' the international community to put pressure on Israel to lift restrictions on humanitarian aid into the Strip, stop the Gaza City offensive, and bring about a pause in fighting leading to the release of the Israseli hostages. 

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Israel intensifies its strikes on Gaza City, expelling civilians and leveling neighborhoods
2:19 Amjad Shawa, Palestinian NGO Network in Deir al-Balah, on drastic situation for civilians seeking to flee Gaza City
5:54 Jacob Granger, Emergency Coordinator, Doctors Without Borders in Deir al-Balah, on near impossibility of treating patients while Israel's onslaught on Gaza City is ongoing


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Was Russia testing NATO's response time in its recent incursion along Estonia's airspace? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>On Friday, three Russian fighter jets entered Estonian airspace without permission, flying over its territory for 12 minutes. Estonian officials raised Article 4 of the NATO treaty, which a NATO member can invoke if they feel that its territorial integrity or security has been threatened. Poland also reported that two Russian jets violated a security zone near a drilling platform in the Baltic Sea. NATO members are set to meet early next week to consider the alliance's response to recent Russian incursions. 

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 NATO members plan to discuss Article 4 triggering on Monday
0:41 Konstantin Eggert, DW Russia Analyst, on Russia's motives for incursion into Estonia's airspace
2:59 Thomas Wiegold, German defense analyst, on whether Russia is gaining tactical information from flying its jets along Estonia's airspace

#estonia #russianjets #natoarticle4

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Estonia involves NATO following Russia fighter jets intrusion into sovereign air space | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump says there could be 'trouble', when he was asked about Russia's latest violation of NATO airspace. The alliance is due to hold talks early next week after Russian military jets entered alliance member Estonia's airspace for 12 minutes on Friday. Moscow denies there was any intrusion. The incident comes just over a week after Russian drones entered Polish airspace, sending tensions between NATO and Russia soaring. 

Chapters:
0:00 Trump's response to Russian intrusion
2:37 Marek Kohv, International Center for Defense and Security
6:02 Jim Townsend, Former US Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense

items.title: Germany seeks Taliban cooperation on Afghan deportations | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>German Interior Minister Alexander Dobrindt has defended his government's direct contacts with the Taliban, arguing they are necessary to carry out deportations. In July, the new German government organized its first flight, sending 81 convicted Afghan offenders back under Taliban rule. The previous government did the same with 28 offenders a year earlier. Both of these agreements had been brokered by Qatar. Now, with direct talks underway, the opposition accuses the government of breaking a political taboo by negotiating with a group that systematically violates human rights, represses women, minorities, and critics.

00:00 Germany seeks Taliban cooperation on Afghan deportations 
01:29 Gerald Knaus, Migration Analyst, European Stability Initiative 


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Pakistan vs India: Fake news battleground? | Global Eyes
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The most recent armed conflict between Pakistan and India saw a new wave of disinformation and AI-generated fake or altered videos as both sides sought the upper hand on the disinformation front. 
DW’s security podcast, Global Eyes, looks at examples from the most recent clash between India and Pakistan with RNW media expert, Surabhi Srivastava, and DW Fact Checking Team Deputy Lead, Rachel Baig. 

#pakistan #india #disinformation

items.title: 'If this was a test from Putin, NATO basically passed it' | To The Point
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Experts see Russia's drone attacks as a revolution in modern warfare. Now, Ukraine is no longer the only country threatened by them. Russian drones have repeatedly entered NATO territory - most recently Poland and Romania. Putin appears to be testing NATO -  continuing to provoke, rather than negotiate. His drone arsenal is posing an ever-greater challenge to NATO. How should the alliance respond to this new threat? We ask: Putin’s drone war - NATO's worst nightmare?

Our guests: 
Marcus Welsch (journalist and data analyst)
Ulrike Franke (ECFR)
Aaron Wiener (Washington Post)

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
